message:
feat(cli): add perf guardrails gate, SQLite open fallback, and robot/CI enhancements

Adds a new "Perf migration guardrails" CI gate (16th gate) that validates
native-vs-legacy command surface latency budgets. The perf_guardrails.rs test
measures p95 latency for migrated CLI help surfaces (ci, bench, golden verify,
flake-triage, check-inbox, serve-http, e2e run, share) and compares against
configured budgets and optional legacy baselines. When a legacy script is
unavailable, a deterministic "legacy unavailable" rationale is recorded rather
than failing.

The performance gate logic now aggregates status across both "Perf + security
regressions" and "Perf migration guardrails" gates using a status_of_many
helper that applies fail-any/pass-all semantics.

The "E2E full matrix" gate now invokes `am e2e run --project .` instead of
`bash scripts/e2e_test.sh`, completing the migration to the native runner.

SQLite connection opening gains a fallback mechanism: when a relative path
fails with a corruption-class error AND the corresponding absolute path exists,
the CLI transparently falls back to the absolute path. This handles edge cases
where the working directory creates misleading relative paths.

E2E artifact repro commands now reference `am e2e run` as the authoritative
replay path, with repro.env files noting the legacy compatibility fallback.

context.rs: open_conn now delegates to the shared open_db_sync_with_database_url
helper, eliminating duplicated schema-init logic.

Robot mode gains tests for parse_since_micros validation and outbox navigation,
ensuring sent-message filtering returns correct results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
